tool_input.content:
# CloudWatch to LocalMetrics Migration Plan
# ===============================================
# Rating: 10/10 - Complete systematic approach with comprehensive testing
# Date: 2025-08-22
# Branch: feature/safe-cloudwatch-to-localmetrics-migration
# Safety Branch: safety/cloudwatch-reversion-complete

migration_overview:
  goal: "Safe migration from CloudWatch to LocalMetrics without breaking functionality"
  approach: "DI container root analysis + deep tracing + comprehensive test validation"
  safety_mechanism: "Rollback to safety/cloudwatch-reversion-complete branch"
  validation_strategy: "Dual validation - DI container integration + module unit tests"

phase_0_baseline_validation:
  primary_goal: "Create bulletproof DI container test BEFORE any changes"
  location: "Project root (not tests/integration/)"
  file: "test_di_container_baseline.py"
  requirements:
    - "Single file that iterates until DI container fully initializes"
    - "Use existing project root venv"
    - "AWS_PROFILE=campaign_prod_v7 environment"
    - "PYTHONPATH=. (project root)"
    - "Install missing dependencies as discovered"
    - "Access to AWS Secrets Manager for real tokens"
    
  validation_criteria:
    - "✅ get_container() succeeds completely"
    - "✅ cloudwatch_metrics service retrieved successfully"
    - "✅ FeedbackReactionsHandler creates without error"
    - "✅ publish_feedback_metric() method callable"
    - "✅ All factory functions execute without exceptions"

  gold_standard_tests:
    unit_tests: "pytest tests/unit/cloud/test_metrics.py -v"
    integration_tests: "pytest tests/integration/ -k cloudwatch -v"
    feedback_tests: "pytest tests/unit/slack/interactive_elements/test_feedback_reactions.py -v"
    factory_tests: "pytest tests/unit/core/client_factory/test_slack_factory.py -v"

phase_1_deep_analysis:
  agents: 
    - "ketchup-expert"
    - "tech-research-agent"
  methodology: "DI container root + comprehensive tracing"
  deliverables:
    dependency_graph: "Visual map of CloudWatch usage"
    factory_analysis: "Every _create_*() function using CloudWatch"
    registration_mapping: "DI container keys and CloudWatch dependencies"
    runtime_tracing: "Constructor calls, method invocations, parameter flows"
    interface_specification: "Exact API CloudWatch provides to each consumer"
    test_mapping: "Which unit/integration tests cover each CloudWatch usage"

phase_2_interface_bridge_design:
  agents: 
    - "python-expert"
    - "ketchup-refactoring-specialist"
  strategy: "Create LocalMetrics interface matching CloudWatch API exactly"
  implementation:
    - "LocalMetricsAdapter class with identical method signatures"
    - "Type hints matching CloudWatch exactly"
    - "Async/await compatibility"  
    - "Error handling patterns matching CloudWatch"
    - "Feature flag for gradual migration (KETCHUP_USE_LOCAL_METRICS)"
  
  test_validation_after_design:
    unit_tests: "Create tests/unit/core/test_local_metrics_adapter.py"
    compatibility_tests: "Verify LocalMetrics matches CloudWatch interface exactly"
    baseline_test: "DI container baseline test still passes"

phase_3_incremental_migration:
  agents: 
    - "ketchup-refactoring-specialist"
    - "python-expert"
    - "test-automation-fixer"
  approach: "Factory-by-factory replacement with comprehensive validation"
  
  step_3a_local_metrics_service:
    changes: "Update integration_factory.py - add LocalMetrics service"
    test_validation:
      unit_tests: "pytest tests/unit/core/client_factory/test_integration_factory.py -v"
      integration_tests: "pytest tests/integration/test_di_container_initialization.py -v"
      baseline_test: "test_di_container_baseline.py passes"
  
  step_3b_feedback_handler_bridge:
    changes: "Modify feedback_reactions.py to accept both CloudWatch and LocalMetrics"
    test_validation:
      unit_tests: "pytest tests/unit/slack/interactive_elements/test_feedback_reactions.py -v"
      integration_tests: "pytest tests/integration/test_feedback_button_functionality.py -v"
      factory_tests: "pytest tests/unit/core/client_factory/test_slack_factory.py -v"
      baseline_test: "test_di_container_baseline.py passes"
  
  step_3c_di_container_switch:
    changes: "Switch DI container to use LocalMetrics with feature flag"
    test_validation:
      all_unit_tests: "pytest tests/unit/cloud/ tests/unit/slack/interactive_elements/ -v"
      all_integration_tests: "pytest tests/integration/test_feedback_functionality.py -v"
      di_container_tests: "pytest tests/unit/core/test_di_container.py -v"
      baseline_test: "test_di_container_baseline.py passes with LocalMetrics"
  
  step_3d_gradual_removal:
    changes: "Remove CloudWatch dependencies incrementally"
    test_validation_per_removal:
      affected_unit_tests: "Run tests for each modified module"
      affected_integration_tests: "Run integration tests for affected services"
      regression_suite: "Full test suite after each removal"
      baseline_test: "Always passes throughout process"

phase_4_validation_automation:
  agents: 
    - "test-automation-fixer"
    - "code-reviewer"
    - "debugger"
  
  after_each_change_validation_sequence:
    1_baseline_test: "test_di_container_baseline.py MUST pass"
    2_unit_tests: "pytest tests/unit/ -x --tb=short (for modified modules)"
    3_integration_tests: "pytest tests/integration/ -x --tb=short (for affected services)"
    4_feedback_validation: "Specific feedback button functionality tests"
    5_code_review: "Security, maintainability, type safety review"
    6_performance_check: "LocalMetrics vs CloudWatch performance comparison"
  
  continuous_monitoring:
    test_coverage: "Ensure no reduction in test coverage during migration"
    performance_metrics: "Track LocalMetrics performance vs CloudWatch baseline"
    error_tracking: "Monitor for new exceptions or failures"

phase_5_cleanup_verification:
  final_validation_suite:
    baseline_test: "test_di_container_baseline.py passes with LocalMetrics only"
    complete_unit_suite: "pytest tests/unit/ --cov=packages --cov-report=term-missing"
    complete_integration_suite: "pytest tests/integration/ -v --tb=short"
    feedback_functionality: "End-to-end feedback button test in DM and channels"
    no_cloudwatch_imports: "grep -r 'CloudWatchMetrics' packages/ returns empty"
    production_readiness: "All tests pass, no CloudWatch dependencies remain"
  
  rollback_capability_verification:
    safety_branch_test: "git checkout safety/cloudwatch-reversion-complete && test_di_container_baseline.py passes"
    migration_branch_test: "Current branch passes all tests with LocalMetrics"
    deployment_test: "Docker build succeeds, no CloudWatch errors in logs"

agent_orchestration:
  concurrent_usage:
    analysis_phase: "ketchup-expert + tech-research-agent (parallel)"
    design_phase: "python-expert + ketchup-refactoring-specialist (collaborative)"
    implementation: "Sequential with comprehensive test validation after each change"
  
  validation_chain_after_every_change:
    immediate: "test-automation-fixer (run affected tests)"
    quality: "code-reviewer (security, maintainability)"
    debugging: "debugger (if any test failures occur)"
    baseline: "Always verify test_di_container_baseline.py passes"

testing_strategy_gold_standard:
  dual_validation:
    di_container_test: "Proves entire system integration works"
    module_unit_tests: "Proves individual components work correctly"
  
  test_execution_pattern:
    before_any_changes: "Establish baseline - all tests pass with CloudWatch"
    after_each_change: "Module unit tests + affected integration tests + baseline DI test"
    continuous_regression: "Full test suite after major changes"
    final_validation: "Complete test suite + baseline + production readiness"

risk_mitigation:
  test_failure_protocol:
    unit_test_failure: "Stop migration, fix issue, re-run full affected test suite"
    integration_test_failure: "Stop migration, analyze dependency impact, fix"
    baseline_test_failure: "CRITICAL - rollback change immediately, investigate"
  
  safety_mechanisms:
    feature_flag: "KETCHUP_USE_LOCAL_METRICS for gradual rollout"
    rollback_branch: "safety/cloudwatch-reversion-complete always available"
    test_gate: "No change proceeds without passing test validation"

strengths:
  comprehensive_testing_coverage:
    - "DI container integration + module unit tests"
    - "Tests after every single change"
    - "Full test suite validation at key milestones"
    - "Rollback validation capability verified"
  
  systematic_approach:
    - "DI container root + deep tracing methodology"
    - "Step-by-step with validation gates"
    - "LocalMetrics exactly matches CloudWatch API"
    - "Right specialists for each phase"
  
  risk_elimination:
    - "Know current state works perfectly"
    - "Clear procedures for handling issues"
    - "Gradual rollout mechanism"
    - "Safety branch + test validation"

minimal_remaining_weaknesses:
  execution_complexity:
    - "Comprehensive testing adds execution time"
    - "Multiple agents and validation steps coordination"
    - "Requires proper AWS setup"
  
  manageable_risks:
    - "Local vs production environment differences"
    - "Package version compatibility"
    - "Minimal AWS cost from real calls during testing"

immediate_next_steps:
  1: "Create test_di_container_baseline.py in project root"
  2: "Ensure it passes with current CloudWatch system"
  3: "Only then proceed to Phase 1 deep analysis"
  
success_criteria:
  - "Feedback buttons work in DM and public channels"
  - "No CloudWatch dependencies remain"
  - "All unit and integration tests pass"
  - "LocalMetrics provides equivalent functionality"
  - "Performance is maintained or improved"
  - "Rollback capability verified and working"

tool_input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
DI Container Baseline Test - Phase 0 of CloudWatch to LocalMetrics Migration

This is the gold standard test that MUST PASS before any CloudWatch migration begins.
It validates that the complete DI container initializes successfully with all services.

Requirements from migration plan:
- Single file that iterates until DI container fully initializes 
- Use existing project root venv
- AWS_PROFILE=campaign_prod_v7 environment
- PYTHONPATH=. (project root)  
- Install missing dependencies as discovered
- Access to AWS Secrets Manager for real tokens

Validation Criteria:
✅ get_container() succeeds completely
✅ cloudwatch_metrics service retrieved successfully  
✅ FeedbackReactionsHandler creates without error
✅ publish_feedback_metric() method callable
✅ All factory functions execute without exceptions

Usage:
    export AWS_PROFILE=campaign_prod_v7
    export PYTHONPATH=.
    python test_di_container_baseline.py

This test documents the exact working state before migration begins.
"""

import os
import sys
import asyncio
import traceback
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional


class BaselineTestRunner:
    """Manages the baseline test execution and validation"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.required_env = {
            'AWS_PROFILE': 'campaign_prod_v7',
            'AWS_REGION': 'eu-west-1', 
            'DYNAMODB_TABLE_NAME': 'ketchup_channel_information',
            'AWS_SECRET_NAME': 'Ketchup_Token_Secrets',
            'LOG_LEVEL': 'INFO',
            'PYTHONPATH': '.'
        }
        self.original_env = {}
        self.container = None
        self.validation_results = {}
        
    def validate_environment(self) -> bool:
        """Validate required environment variables are set"""
        print("🔧 Phase 0 Step 1: Environment Validation")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        missing_vars = []
        for key, expected_value in self.required_env.items():
            current_value = os.environ.get(key)
            if key == 'AWS_PROFILE' and current_value != expected_value:
                missing_vars.append(f"{key} (expected: {expected_value}, got: {current_value})")
            elif not current_value:
                missing_vars.append(key)
            else:
                print(f"✅ {key}={current_value}")
        
        if missing_vars:
            print("\n❌ Missing or incorrect environment variables:")
            for var in missing_vars:
                print(f"   {var}")
            print("\nPlease set required environment variables:")
            print("   export AWS_PROFILE=campaign_prod_v7")  
            print("   export PYTHONPATH=.")
            return False
            
        return True

    def setup_environment(self) -> None:
        """Setup environment for baseline test"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 2: Environment Setup")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        # Store original values for restoration
        for key, value in self.required_env.items():
            self.original_env[key] = os.environ.get(key)
            os.environ[key] = value
            print(f"📝 Set {key}={value}")

    async def test_di_container_initialization(self) -> bool:
        """Test complete DI container initialization"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 3: DI Container Initialization")  
        print("=" * 50)
        
        try:
            print("   Importing DI container modules...")
            from packages.core.di_container import get_container, cleanup_container
            from packages.core.client_factory_utils import get_instance
            
            print("   Initializing COMPLETE DI container (all services)...")
            print("   This includes: secrets, db, integration, cloud, slack, ai, langchain")
            
            self.container = await get_container()
            print("✅ FULL DI Container initialized successfully")
            
            self.validation_results['di_container_init'] = True
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ DI Container initialization failed: {e}")
            print(f"   Stack trace: {traceback.format_exc()}")
            self.validation_results['di_container_init'] = False
            return False

    async def test_service_availability(self) -> bool:
        """Test availability of all major service categories"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 4: Service Availability Test")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        try:
            from packages.core.client_factory_utils import get_instance
            
            # Core services that must be available
            critical_services = [
                ("secrets_manager", "Secrets Manager"),
                ("dynamodb_store", "Database"),
                ("cloudwatch_metrics", "CloudWatch Metrics"), 
                ("slack_posting", "Slack Client"),
                ("azure_openai_client", "Azure OpenAI"),
                ("langchain_llm", "LangChain LLM")
            ]
            
            all_critical_available = True
            
            print("   Testing critical services:")
            for service_key, service_name in critical_services:
                service = get_instance(service_key)
                available = service is not None
                status = "✅" if available else "❌"
                service_type = type(service).__name__ if service else 'None'
                print(f"   {status} {service_name}: {service_key} -> {service_type}")
                
                if not available:
                    all_critical_available = False
                    
            self.validation_results['service_availability'] = all_critical_available
            return all_critical_available
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Service availability test failed: {e}")
            self.validation_results['service_availability'] = False
            return False

    async def test_cloudwatch_metrics_integration(self) -> bool:
        """Test CloudWatch metrics service specifically"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 5: CloudWatch Metrics Integration")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        try:
            from packages.core.client_factory_utils import get_instance
            
            print("   Retrieving CloudWatch metrics service...")
            cloudwatch_metrics = get_instance("cloudwatch_metrics")
            
            if cloudwatch_metrics is None:
                print("❌ CloudWatch metrics service not available")
                self.validation_results['cloudwatch_integration'] = False
                return False
                
            print(f"✅ CloudWatch metrics service type: {type(cloudwatch_metrics)}")
            
            # Inspect interface
            print("   Validating CloudWatch interface...")
            has_put_metric = hasattr(cloudwatch_metrics, 'put_metric')
            print(f"   put_metric method available: {'✅' if has_put_metric else '❌'}")
            
            if has_put_metric:
                print(f"   CloudWatch methods: {[m for m in dir(cloudwatch_metrics) if not m.startswith('_')]}")
            
            self.validation_results['cloudwatch_integration'] = has_put_metric
            return has_put_metric
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ CloudWatch integration test failed: {e}")
            self.validation_results['cloudwatch_integration'] = False
            return False

    async def test_feedback_handler_creation(self) -> bool:
        """Test FeedbackReactionsHandler creation - the critical component that broke"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 6: FeedbackReactionsHandler Creation")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        try:
            print("   Testing FeedbackReactionsHandler factory function...")
            from packages.core.client_factory.slack_factory import _create_feedback_reactions_handler
            
            feedback_handler = await _create_feedback_reactions_handler()
            
            if feedback_handler is None:
                print("❌ FeedbackReactionsHandler creation returned None")
                self.validation_results['feedback_handler_creation'] = False
                return False
                
            print("✅ FeedbackReactionsHandler created successfully")
            print(f"   Handler type: {type(feedback_handler)}")
            
            # Validate it has CloudWatch metrics
            has_cloudwatch = hasattr(feedback_handler, '_cloudwatch_metrics')
            print(f"   Has CloudWatch metrics: {'✅' if has_cloudwatch else '❌'}")
            
            self.validation_results['feedback_handler_creation'] = has_cloudwatch
            return has_cloudwatch
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ FeedbackReactionsHandler creation failed: {e}")
            print(f"   Stack trace: {traceback.format_exc()}")
            self.validation_results['feedback_handler_creation'] = False
            return False

    async def test_publish_feedback_metric(self) -> bool:
        """Test the exact method that broke during CloudWatch removal"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 7: publish_feedback_metric Test")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        try:
            from packages.core.client_factory.slack_factory import _create_feedback_reactions_handler
            
            print("   Creating FeedbackReactionsHandler...")
            feedback_handler = await _create_feedback_reactions_handler()
            
            if feedback_handler is None:
                print("❌ Cannot test publish_feedback_metric - handler creation failed")
                self.validation_results['publish_metric_callable'] = False
                return False
            
            print("   Testing publish_feedback_metric method (REAL CloudWatch call)...")
            # This will actually publish to CloudWatch - exactly what we need to test
            result = await feedback_handler.publish_feedback_metric("baseline_test", 1)
            
            print(f"✅ publish_feedback_metric REAL result: {result}")
            print("   This proves the complete integration works end-to-end")
            
            self.validation_results['publish_metric_callable'] = True
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ publish_feedback_metric test failed: {e}")
            print(f"   Stack trace: {traceback.format_exc()}")
            self.validation_results['publish_metric_callable'] = False
            return False

    async def test_factory_functions(self) -> bool:
        """Test that all major factory functions execute without exceptions"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 8: Factory Functions Validation")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        factory_functions = [
            ("slack_factory", "_create_feedback_reactions_handler"),
            ("integration_factory", "_create_cloudwatch_metrics"),
            # Add more critical factories as needed
        ]
        
        all_factories_work = True
        
        try:
            for module_name, function_name in factory_functions:
                try:
                    print(f"   Testing {module_name}.{function_name}...")
                    
                    if module_name == "slack_factory":
                        from packages.core.client_factory.slack_factory import _create_feedback_reactions_handler
                        result = await _create_feedback_reactions_handler()
                        success = result is not None
                    elif module_name == "integration_factory":
                        from packages.core.client_factory.integration_factory import _create_cloudwatch_metrics
                        result = await _create_cloudwatch_metrics()  
                        success = result is not None
                    else:
                        success = False
                        
                    status = "✅" if success else "❌"
                    print(f"   {status} {module_name}.{function_name}")
                    
                    if not success:
                        all_factories_work = False
                        
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"   ❌ {module_name}.{function_name} failed: {e}")
                    all_factories_work = False
                    
            self.validation_results['factory_functions'] = all_factories_work
            return all_factories_work
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Factory functions test failed: {e}")
            self.validation_results['factory_functions'] = False
            return False

    def generate_baseline_report(self) -> None:
        """Generate comprehensive baseline report"""
        print("\n🔧 Phase 0 Step 9: Baseline Report Generation")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        total_tests = len(self.validation_results)
        passed_tests = sum(1 for result in self.validation_results.values() if result)
        
        print(f"\n📊 BASELINE TEST RESULTS: {passed_tests}/{total_tests} PASSED")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        for test_name, passed in self.validation_results.items():
            status = "✅ PASS" if passed else "❌ FAIL"
            print(f"{status} {test_name}")
        
        if passed_tests == total_tests:
            print("\n🎉 ALL BASELINE TESTS PASSED!")
            print("   ✅ Complete DI container initializes successfully")
            print("   ✅ CloudWatch metrics integration working") 
            print("   ✅ FeedbackReactionsHandler creation working")
            print("   ✅ publish_feedback_metric() callable and working")
            print("   ✅ All critical factory functions working")
            print("\n✨ READY FOR CLOUDWATCH TO LOCALMETRICS MIGRATION")
        else:
            print(f"\n💥 {total_tests - passed_tests} BASELINE TESTS FAILED!")
            print("   ❌ CANNOT PROCEED WITH MIGRATION UNTIL ALL TESTS PASS")
            print("   ❌ FIX FAILING TESTS BEFORE CONTINUING")

    def restore_environment(self) -> None:
        """Restore original environment"""
        print("\n🧹 Environment Restoration")
        print("=" * 50)
        
        for key, original_value in self.original_env.items():
            if original_value is None:
                os.environ.pop(key, None)
                print(f"🗑️  Removed {key}")
            else:
                os.environ[key] = original_value
                print(f"↩️  Restored {key}={original_value}")

    async def cleanup_resources(self) -> None:
        """Cleanup DI container and resources"""
        if self.container:
            print("\n🧹 DI Container Cleanup")
            print("=" * 50)
            try:
                from packages.core.di_container import cleanup_container
                await cleanup_container()
                print("✅ DI container cleanup completed")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"⚠️  DI container cleanup warning: {e}")

    async def run_complete_baseline_test(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete baseline test suite"""
        print("🧪 DI CONTAINER BASELINE TEST - PHASE 0")
        print("CloudWatch to LocalMetrics Migration Validation")
        print("=" * 60)
        
        try:
            # Step 1: Environment validation
            if not self.validate_environment():
                return False
                
            # Step 2: Environment setup  
            self.setup_environment()
            
            # Step 3: DI container initialization
            if not await self.test_di_container_initialization():
                return False
                
            # Step 4: Service availability
            if not await self.test_service_availability():
                return False
                
            # Step 5: CloudWatch integration
            if not await self.test_cloudwatch_metrics_integration():
                return False
                
            # Step 6: Feedback handler creation
            if not await self.test_feedback_handler_creation():
                return False
                
            # Step 7: Publish metric test
            if not await self.test_publish_feedback_metric():
                return False
                
            # Step 8: Factory functions
            if not await self.test_factory_functions():
                return False
                
            # Step 9: Generate report
            self.generate_baseline_report()
            
            # All tests passed
            return all(self.validation_results.values())
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"💥 Baseline test suite failed: {e}")
            print(f"Stack trace: {traceback.format_exc()}")
            return False
            
        finally:
            # Always cleanup
            await self.cleanup_resources()
            self.restore_environment()


async def main():
    """Main entry point for baseline test"""
    
    # Pre-flight checks
    if os.environ.get('AWS_PROFILE') != 'campaign_prod_v7':
        print("❌ AWS_PROFILE must be set to 'campaign_prod_v7'")
        print("   Run: export AWS_PROFILE=campaign_prod_v7")
        sys.exit(1)
        
    if os.environ.get('PYTHONPATH') != '.':
        print("❌ PYTHONPATH must be set to '.' (project root)")
        print("   Run: export PYTHONPATH=.")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    # Run baseline test
    test_runner = BaselineTestRunner()
    success = await test_runner.run_complete_baseline_test()
    
    if success:
        print("\n🚀 BASELINE TEST SUCCESS - READY FOR MIGRATION")
        sys.exit(0)
    else:
        print("\n💥 BASELINE TEST FAILED - CANNOT PROCEED WITH MIGRATION")
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
